limits: Graph-neighborhood candidate integration is disabled by default.
An explicit graph_neighborhood_target_id is required when the feature is enabled.
When enabled, selected graph-neighborhood candidates are added to the ordinary context-pack candidate set and may affect ContextPackManifest.items.
CAP-0009 is a library-level integration capability; standalone CLI exposure was added separately by CAP-0011.
Candidate budgeting uses whole-artifact token estimates and does not provide compact distance-aware representations.
No automatic target inference or automatic graph-neighborhood inclusion is performed.
Does not change provider prompts or prompt rendering.
Does not persist graph state or introduce retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production-index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
Does not by itself complete graph-local scoped memory or close GAP-0002.
name: Opt-in graph-neighborhood context-pack candidates
recommended_next_actions: Use CAP-0011 when explicit standalone context-pack CLI exposure is required.
Evaluate compact distance-aware neighbor representation, isolated-target handling, and local/global context balance before provider-facing or automatic inclusion.
risks: Whole-artifact token estimates can exclude all useful neighbors under small graph token budgets.
Opt-in manifest integration may be mistaken for automatic runtime graph-local memory selection.
Historical CAP-0009 governance metadata contained copied readiness-capability fields; the corrected canonical interpretation is supported by VERIFY-20260716-0004 and WARR-20260716-0005.
summary: Provides a library-level, opt-in and default-off graph-neighborhood context candidate path inside build_latest_context_manifest. When explicitly enabled, read-only graph-neighborhood advisor candidates are converted into ordinary ContextPackItem values and may affect the resulting manifest items. Existing default behavior remains unchanged.
